edge_reviews.notes: Diffusion references support generative modeling; applying them to audio generation is a defensible synthesis, but the packet has no audio-specific diffusion passage.
Transformer sequence-modeling references support the dependency at a broad architecture level, though the packet lacks speech-specific transformer evidence.
Audio signals are intrinsic to speech/audio generation; support is conceptual rather than passage-level in the packet.
Autoregressive generation is plausible for audio token generation, but the packet does not cite direct evidence for this dependency or common strength.
Watermarking is relevant to generated content governance, but the LLM watermark citation does not directly establish audio-generation watermarking or common strength.
C2PA supports provenance for digital content; optional use in generated audio workflows is defensible synthesis.
